description: Accelerated scaling of evergreen open-ended fund programs across PE, infrastructure, and real estate, reaching ~$55B in evergreen AUM (32% of total AUM) by mid-2025. Partners Group launched multiple new evergreen vehicles and expanded existing programs across geographies, including ELTIF-compliant structures in Europe following the ELTIF 2.0 regulation effective January 2024.
stated_rationale: Evergreen programs generate more predictable, recurring management fee revenue compared to traditional drawdown funds with finite fundraising cycles. The semi-liquid format appeals to private wealth clients who require periodic liquidity windows, enabling Partners Group to access a structurally larger capital pool. Management has stated that evergreen is the natural vehicle format for private markets and expects it to become the majority of AUM over time.
observable_metric_impact: Evergreen AUM grew from ~$30B (2022) to ~$55B (H1 2025), representing 32% of total AUM. Management fee margins on evergreen products are comparable to drawdown funds (~1.3-1.5% blended). Evergreen programs contributed to 18% YoY growth in management fees in FY2024. Client count in evergreen vehicles exceeded 200,000 by end-2024.
op_prereq_summary: Requires robust NAV calculation and valuation infrastructure for semi-liquid vehicles, distribution partnerships with global private banks and wealth platforms, and regulatory approval for ELTIF/interval fund structures across jurisdictions.

description: Systematic expansion of private wealth distribution channel through partnerships with major global banks, wealth managers, and retail distribution platforms. Partners Group built out dedicated private wealth teams in key markets (US, Europe, Asia-Pacific) and secured distribution agreements with leading wirehouses and private banks to place evergreen and drawdown products with high-net-worth and affluent investors.
stated_rationale: The institutional fundraising market is increasingly competitive with compressed margins, while the private wealth segment represents a multi-trillion-dollar opportunity with higher fee tolerance and lower concentration risk. Management has emphasized that private wealth is the firm's largest growth vector and that the channel requires purpose-built products (evergreen format), dedicated client servicing, and education infrastructure distinct from institutional distribution.
observable_metric_impact: Private wealth client AUM grew from ~$25B (2022) to an estimated ~$45B by end-2024, driven by evergreen inflows. The number of distribution partnerships with banks and platforms expanded to over 50 globally. Private wealth represented an increasing share of gross new inflows, rising from ~30% in 2022 to ~40% in 2024. Revenue per private wealth mandate is accretive to blended fee rates.
op_prereq_summary: Requires dedicated private wealth distribution teams in each market, integration with bank and wealth-platform technology infrastructure for subscription and reporting, and ELTIF/interval fund regulatory wrappers suited to non-institutional investors.

description: Deepened direct investing platform capabilities by increasing the share of direct equity and direct debt transactions originated and managed in-house, reducing reliance on fund-of-funds and co-investment intermediation. By 2024, over 90% of new investments were made directly (direct equity, direct lending, direct infrastructure) rather than through third-party fund commitments. Partners Group also introduced private markets royalties as a fifth asset class in 2023, structuring contractual royalty streams from portfolio companies as a distinct investable strategy.
stated_rationale: Direct investing provides greater control over value creation, entry pricing, and exit timing compared to fund-of-funds or LP commitments. It also supports higher management and performance fee structures. The royalties initiative represents a differentiated product innovation — monetizing contractual revenue streams from portfolio companies as a new return source uncorrelated with traditional PE/credit cycles. Management positioned royalties as a natural extension of the direct investing platform, leveraging existing deal flow and operational capabilities.
observable_metric_impact: Direct investments represented >90% of new commitments by 2024, up from ~80% in 2020. Performance fees (carried interest) from direct deals were the primary driver of performance fee revenue, which totaled CHF 564M in FY2024. The royalties strategy launched with initial commitments of approximately $2-3B, primarily sourced from existing evergreen vehicles. Total AUM reached CHF 149.2B at end-2024 (reported in CHF), with direct investing driving higher blended management fee rates versus legacy fund-of-funds mandates.
op_prereq_summary: Requires deep in-house deal sourcing, due diligence, and portfolio operations teams across asset classes globally; proprietary valuation and structuring capabilities for royalty streams; and existing portfolio company relationships to originate royalty-linked instruments.
